_rationale: index_stats with no arguments — default-arg dispatch path. Iter-8 (DART-vJrTKPxyk7RF): C++ MCP index_stats handler fixed to emit Go-shape payload — `timestamp` (RFC3339Nano string) replaces `timestamp_ms` (int), and `total_size_bytes` (sum of FileContentStore content sizes) is now emitted. Both sides now produce structurally identical bodies: {file_count, index_time_ms, reference_count, server_ready, status, symbol_count, timestamp, total_size_bytes}. The parity_runner normalize_mcp_inner_text ignore_paths list (parity_runner.cpp:73-97) drops timestamp + total_size_bytes + reference_count + symbol_count + progress runtime fields and canonicalises key order, so the inner text body of both binaries collapses to the same canonical JSON after normalisation. Probe: /tmp/iter8/cpp_post.json vs /tmp/iter8/go.json — keys identical, types match. Text body lifted from ignore→stable; iter-7 ignore tier was the residual workaround for the timestamp_ms/total_size_bytes shape gap closed in iter-8.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope + inner text body. content[].type='text' locks the frame; content[].text is normalised through normalize_mcp_inner_text which strips the volatile fields (timestamp, total_size_bytes, reference_count, symbol_count, progress runtime stats) and canonicalises key order — leaving status, server_ready, and structural shape under parity guard. Single-shot Go async vs C++ sync timing for the `status` field is absorbed by the runner's normaliser (no progress.* leak) and by Go's fast sync corpus completion on the 4-file multi-lang corpus.
tiers._rationale_ignore: Envelope-level non-deterministic fields only — JSON-RPC id mirrors the request, jsonrpc is constant, meta.elapsed_ms is wall-clock, meta.server_pid is per-process. Inner text body promoted to stable in iter-8 after the timestamp/total_size_bytes shape fix.
